title: Do you think we should make AI trading bot platform?
author: Traditional_Ear5237
selftext:
Hey, just want to launch a discussion in the community here. We are a research AI team focusing on financial markets. Over the last 2 years we have developed pretty advanced AI agents which on autopilot trade cryptocurrency. Overall we have 1300 of those agents in deployment currently. Slowly building a fund. Some team members proposed that perhaps we could democratize some of our older agent technology for the general public in a form of a platform where users can come in, select their agents, connect their exchange, tweak a few settings on the agents and this way automate their trading.

For a simple intro, our agents consist of 2 components, a brain which is just an ML (deep learning, traditional ML models etc.), and the behavioral policy. Think of behavioral policy as a program that is specifically tailored to each brain. It decides when and how to enter, exist positions, where to put the stop loss, trailing stop loss, breakeven stop loss, and around 8 other behavioral parameters. 

So what we are trying to understand is if people would even want that? It does not cost us much to build that and launch to the public, but we are not sure that there is demand for such a service. From what we see the market is currently filled with pretty much either Grid bots or DCA bots from different companies. If you have used these, can you share what you like about them, what you don’t like about them. And if you could have the perfect trading automation product what would it looks like, what features would it have and…yeah, anything you want to describe really.

Just trying to launch a discussion to see what people do these days and how you automate your trading if at all, and what are the common problems you guys face. In either case, let’s talk.
